title: impatiens cuspidata subsp. cuspidata
intro: Impatiens cuspidata subsp. cuspidata, also known as Balsam Apple or Chinese Balsam, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
